page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture using the AWS Neoverse-N1 model running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and the Nitro hypervisor. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6gn.large demonstrates moderate single-core capabilities but overall lower multi-core and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance registers at 1515.29, placing it in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3014.06 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1749.0. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 38.34 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 18.72 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES, though other advanced vector and matrix extensions are absent.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gn.large offers a dedicated ARM-based compute profile suitable for entry-level network-optimized workloads without the overhead of larger instances. Given its 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and dedicated vCPUs, it is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and simple utility computing tasks that benefit from dedicated ARM cores but do not require massive memory footprints or high-performance multi-threaded processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is a compute-optimized, arm64-based instance featuring 2 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of memory, a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1515.29, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level, network-optimized workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and simple utility computing tasks that benefit from dedicated ARM cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight web serving.

tagline: A dedicated arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring Graviton processors and baseline network performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated arm64 cores
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps
Delivers average single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving and basic database operations
